question_text: A student investigates different types of forces by testing a magnet near a paperclip, pulling a rope, rubbing sandpaper on a block, and dropping a ball. Which of the following correctly identifies one contact force and one non-contact force from these examples?

options.label: C
options.text: Contact: gravity; Non-contact: pulling a rope

explanation: Friction (sandpaper rubbing) is a contact force because surfaces must touch. Magnetism (magnet near paperclip) is a non-contact force because the magnet attracts the paperclip without touching it. Tension (rope) and gravity (falling ball) are both forces but require careful classification.
